summary: Backend-only testing of Razorpay payment routes after migration from Supabase Edge Functions to FastAPI. All 19 pytest tests pass: health check, auth gating (401 without/with-invalid bearer on all 4 endpoints), token-purchase create-order validations (tokens-too-low/high, amount-below-min, price-mismatch, valid create), token-purchase verify (invalid signature 400, valid HMAC signature credits tokens via credit_user_tokens RPC, idempotent 'already processed' on replay), and plan-checkout (404 for unknown plan UUID, 400 for invalid signature). Razorpay test order was successfully created against rzp_test_SpjjvTzWU5fO6F (amount 100000 paise = INR 1000 for 1M tokens). Signature verification HMAC math confirmed correct end-to-end (synthetic payment_id with real HMAC succeeded). credit_user_tokens RPC working - tokens_credited > 0 returned on verify.

minor.issue: Because auth is checked inside the handler body (not via FastAPI Depends/Security), requests with empty/invalid JSON body return 422 (Pydantic validation) before reaching the 401 auth check. Real frontend clients always send valid bodies so they correctly receive 401, but unauthenticated probing/fuzzing will see 422 instead of 401. Consider extracting _get_user_from_token into a FastAPI dependency so auth runs first.

If the custom_token_purchases insert silently fails (caught & logged), the endpoint still returns success with purchase_id=null. Verify path then 404s with 'Purchase record not found'. In tests the insert succeeded, but this swallowed error could surface in production. Consider failing the request when the insert fails.

action_items: (Optional) Convert _get_user_from_token into a FastAPI dependency so 401 fires before Pydantic 422 on bad bodies.
(Optional) Surface DB insert failures in /token-purchase/create-order instead of returning purchase_id=null and silently logging.
critical_code_review_comments: auth is performed inside handler, not as a FastAPI dependency - leads to 422 before 401 on malformed requests.
custom_token_purchases insert errors are swallowed (try/except Exception, log only) - downstream verify will 404 with confusing 'Purchase record not found'.
_resolve_plan_price falls through to plan.price_inr for any months value not in {3,6,12} including 1 - intended behaviour but undocumented.
PLAN_TOKENS_PER_MONTH is hardcoded by plan_key; if a new plan_key is added in DB it silently falls back to plan.ai_tokens_monthly. OK but worth a code comment.
razorpay_client is module-level (created at import). RAZORPAY_KEY_ID/_SECRET are required via os.environ[...] - app will crash at import if missing (good fail-fast).
/api/payment/token-purchase/verify accepts any payment_id text - relies entirely on HMAC. This matches Razorpay's documented webhook verification pattern and is correct.
test_credentials: Fresh Supabase user signed up per-run via /auth/v1/signup with email e2e+<hex>@avatartalk-test.io. Email confirmation is currently DISABLED (signup returns access_token directly).
seed_data_creation: Each test run creates 1 Supabase auth user + 1 custom_token_purchases row (status=completed) + tokens credited via credit_user_tokens RPC. No cleanup performed (test data is namespaced by random email).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Run `pytest /app/backend/tests/test_payment_routes.py -v`. The signup fixture is session-scoped; if Supabase later enforces email confirmation, the fixture will pytest.skip with a clear message. Real Razorpay orders are created on each run (test mode, no charge). To test the plan-checkout happy path, seed a row in platform_pricing_plans and pass its id as planId with billingCycleMonths in {1,3,6,12}.
rca of the issue: Original 'failed to create payment order' was because frontend was calling Supabase Edge Functions that were never deployed (no Supabase CLI in container). Migration to FastAPI /api/payment/* routes is now functional end-to-end: Razorpay order creation returns valid order_id, HMAC verification accepts correct signatures and rejects invalid ones, credit_user_tokens RPC credits tokens, and idempotency replay returns 'Already processed'.
